facts: The patient's name is Bryan.
The patient was raking leaves in his yard yesterday.
The patient felt fine while raking leaves.
The patient's back started tightening up about two hours after raking leaves.
The patient experienced pins and needles in his right foot.
The patient had back pain like this once before, about three years ago.
The previous back pain went away after a day.
The patient put ice on his back.
The patient took two Advils for the pain.
The Advils did not help the pain.
The patient's back feels a little better when he sits.

The patient normally does not do physical activity.
The patient's back hurts a little when he bends over.
The patient has no pain when standing up from bending over.
The patient has pain to palpation on his lower back.
The pain is around the L5 area.
The patient's partner is present during the consultation.
The patient's partner reports that the patient's pain is worse when standing up.
The patient's partner is worried about the patient's back pain.
The patient's partner thinks the patient's pain tolerance is high.
The patient did not actually rake leaves last week, but thought about it and did not experience much back pain.
This time, the patient's back pain is worse.

The X-ray results are normal.
The doctor thinks the patient probably sprained his lower back.
